content.full_text:
Fnrn Polrrrcer- PRACTTcEs LouMrssroN
;X28 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento. CA 9-581"1-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
July 16,2008
Christina Avila
Campaign Disclosure Filing Officer
Orange County Registrar of Voters
1300 South Grand Avenue, Bldg. C
Santa Ana, CA 92705
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-08-115
Dear Ms. Avila:
This letter is in response to your.request for advice regarding campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Since your inquiry is general in nature, we are
treating your request as one for informal assistance.2 I confirmed with you that yolr are
asking about deadlines for statements filed by candidates and ballot measure committees.
QUESTIONS
1. May the second pre-election campaign disclosure statement be mailed on the
due date by overnight delivery. received after the due date, and still be considered filed
on time?
2. May the second pre-election campaign disclosure statement be faxed on the
due date and still be considered filed on time if the mailed original is received after the
due date?
' The Political Refirrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All
statutory ret'erences are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0through 18997 of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2. Division 6 of the California Code rlf
Regulations. unless otherwise indicated.
: Infirrmal assistance clles not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
tirrmal written advice. (Section 83 I l4: regulation I 8329(c X3 ), copy enclosed. )


File No. I-08-115
Page No.2
3. How many days does a committee have to file the original second pre-election
campaign disclosure statement if a copy was faxed to you on the due date?
4. Would a faxed second pre-election campaign disclosure statement fulfill the
filing deadline requirement?
5. If the second pre-election campaign disclosure statement is faxed, could the
original be mailed by first class? If not, what type of mail service must the committee
use?
CONCLUSIONS
1. Yes. A statement is considered filed on the date it is received by the delivery
servlce.
2. Yes. A statement may be faxed by the applicable deadline provided that the
original is sent by first-class mail or any other personal delivery or guaranteed overnight
delivery service within 24 hours of the applicable deadline.
3. See the conclusion to question 2.
4. No. A statement faxed by the applicable deadline must be followed up with an
original in order to meet the filing requirements. The original must be sent by first-class
mail or any other personal delivery or guaranteed overnight delivery service within 24
hours of the applicable deadline.
5. Yes
ANALYSIS
Government Code Sections 84200J and 84200.8 require the second pre-election
statement filed by candidate controlled committees and committees primarily formed to
support or oppose a candidate or measure to be sent by guaranteed overnight delivery
service or delivered in person. Therefore, candidates and committees are required to file
the second pre-election statement by personal delivery on or before the due date or, if
sent by guaranteed overnight delivery service, the delivery service must receive the
statement on or before the due date. For purposes of determining when the statement has
been filed, the statement is considered filed on the date it is received by the delivery
service and not the date the filing officer receives the statement. Reports personally
delivered by a candidate or committee are considered received by the filing officer on the
date of delivery. (Keck Advice Letter, No.I-92-527.)
With respect to faxing statements, Govemment Code Section 81007.5 allows any
campaign statement to be faxed by the applicable deadline, provided that the required
originals or paper copies are sent by first-class mail or by any other personal delivery or
guaranteed overnight delivery service within 24 hours of the applicable deadline and


File No. I-08-115
Page No. 3
provided that the total number of pages of each report or statement faxed is no more than
30 pages. Sections 84200.7 and 84200.8 notwithstanding, because Section 81007.5 does
not make a distinction as to the type of statement being faxed (e.g., first pre-election or
second pre-election), any follow-up method of delivery of the original iJacceptable.
Please note that a faxed statement is not deemed filed if it is not a true and correct
copy of the original statement personally delivered or sent by first-class mail or
guaranteed overnight delivery service. (Section 81007.5(b).)
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me
ar (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
fuj-h
By: Trish Mayer
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
TMjgl
Enclosure
